column1: # Shadow Daimyo — staging rows for merge

column3: description

column1: # Class: court-assassin and spymaster. Etiquette-bound violence; information as currency.
# === L1 Core — fill gaps (3 cards: reaction, mobility, passive) ===

column3: A bow so low it draws no eye, and a word so soft it draws no blood — yet. Until end of turn, your VEIL checks gain an edge and one enemy's next attack roll against you is made as though through courtly politeness: diminished.
Your sandals make no sound on the waxed floor. Move without provoking reactions; if you end adjacent to an enemy, your next EDGE strike this turn gains advantage.

You were trained to kill without raising your voice. While you have Intelligence on a target, your first attack against them each turn cannot trigger alarms, witnesses, or opportunity attacks from others.
Every face you have studied leaves a folded note behind your eyes. Gain 1 Intelligence on any enemy you have seen this encounter, without spending an action to gather it.
You sit below the salt and hear everything. Draw a card; if it is a utility_card, reduce its AP cost by 1 this turn (minimum 0).

column1: # === L3 Spymaster (4) — whisper network, dossiers, eyes-everywhere ===

column3: Your agents are in every shadow of the room. Mark up to three enemies; you see their intended targets and you gain 1 Intelligence on each at start of your next turn.
A single line on rice paper changes the battle. Choose one enemy: their next action is delayed by one turn as an imaginary order reaches them.
The network speaks in code only you can read. Draw two cards; each card drawn this turn converts to 1 Intelligence on a target of your choice instead, if you prefer.

Nothing is said in this room that is not, a moment later, said in yours. For the rest of the encounter, whenever an enemy plays a control_card or utility_card, you learn it and gain 1 Intelligence on them.

column1: # === L3 Court Blade (4) — etiquette-bound duelist, permission to kill ===

column3: The bow is proper. The cut is proper. The target is less sure. EDGE strike that deals additional damage if your last action this turn was not a combat_card.
You name the target, the hour, and the reason. Choose one enemy: until end of encounter, you attack them at advantage and no other enemy may interpose — the duel is sanctioned by the room.
You present your sleeve in apology; the knife waits inside it. When an enemy attacks you and misses, make an immediate EDGE riposte as a reaction.
The ritual duel has fourteen steps. You are skipping to the last. EDGE strike; if the target has not yet acted this round, it costs you 1 AP instead of 2.

column1: # === L3 Broker (4) — trade-power, favors owed, economic leverage ===

column3: Someone owes you. Remove one enemy's minion, ally-of-convenience, or summoned aid from the field until end of round — their master sent them elsewhere at your quiet request.
You open a line of credit against one enemy. Until end of encounter, every attack an ally lands on that target adds 1 Intelligence to your pool.
Everything has a price. Spend 2 Intelligence on a target: their next action becomes yours to direct — choose its target from among their allies.
Trade flows where you say it flows. Move an ally up to their movement distance, then move one enemy half theirs in any direction — a bribed guard, a rerouted supply line, a word in the right ear.

Every whisper you have gathered this encounter exhales at once. Convert all your accumulated Intelligence into a single VEIL+EDGE strike against one enemy; each point of Intelligence sharpens the cut and the cut is heard nowhere.

You pull every thread at once and the tapestry falls. Every enemy with a ledger mark, a favor owed, or Intelligence accumulated on them takes VEIL damage and loses their next reaction — the court remembers what they chose to forget.

There is no chamber in this palace I have not paid a servant to sweep. For the rest of the encounter, all enemies are treated as if you have full Intelligence on them; your strikes ignore cover, concealment, and misdirection.
A message you left for yourself years ago arrives on schedule. Search your discard pile for any utility_card or control_card and play it immediately at 0 AP cost; it was always going to be here.

You produce the sealed order; the room steps back. Make an EDGE strike against your Formal Invitation target with guaranteed hit; if they die, no social, political, or narrative consequence accrues to you — it was sanctioned.
Three bows: to the host, to the opponent, to the blade. Make three EDGE strikes this turn against a single target; each strike between them reduces their resonance by one stage.

You buy the enemy out from under themselves. Choose one enemy minion or summon with fewer hit dice than you: it becomes yours for the remainder of the encounter, its original master unable to countermand you.

Every favor, every bribe, every little kindness you have ever spent returns in a single wave. All ledger-marked and Intelligence-marked enemies lose half their remaining AP this round; you gain 1 AP for each mark consumed.
